steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage’s strongest clue is the phrase “the force of her arguments,” which directly connects to persuasion. This specific wording highlights the power of Marianne’s reasoning to sway others.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice B uses “the force of her arguments” to show how Marianne convinces Willoughby to align with her views through reasoning, matching the claim about persuading others of artistic judgments.

The other three choices describe mutual interests (D), attention-seeking (A), or enthusiasm for discussion (C) but miss the key idea of active persuasion.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
